title: Suaeda nesophila
taxonomy: Suaeda nesophila is classified under the family Amaranthaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the pigweed or goosefoot family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Suaeda belonging to the subfamily Salsoloideae. The genus Suaeda itself is a group of succulent plants that are often found in coastal and saline environments. Suaeda nesophila is a species within this genus and is native to certain regions. It is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Suaeda maritima and Suaeda altissima.
